reviews.review_description: Pretty decent I'd say. I wonder if the rest of the levels were do drab.
still trying  to escape  but love the it all the same .V.R. GOOD

developer_response.body: Thank yoooou! I just updated Twobit so it now shows what Twobit Odyssey (the full version of the game) has in store. This demo now features three levels for free :)

reviews.review_description: Kind of confusing at first but I got it I love this game keep them coming I could play this all day have not passed yet only able to play a little but was fun plain on playing so thought I would give you a heads up
no Russian language

developer_response.body: Sorry, only English is available right now. I will add more languages in the full-on version of the game. But the game can be fully enjoyed without a deep knowledge of English - although the supplied instructions do explain how to start and interact with Twobit.

reviews.review_description: Fantastic! Can't wait for the full version.

developer_response.body: Thank yoooou! I just updated Twobit so it now shows what Twobit Odyssey (the full version of the game) has in store. This demo now features three levels for free :)

reviews.review_description: I found this game to be a healthy, constructive- challenging game. Very well constructed. It needs a bit more attention to detail visually however I'd give this game a 4.999 simply for its genius like unique form of gaming.
Loved playing this hope to see full version really soon. This doesnt need a gamepad just head movement but also not for newbies.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your awesome feedback! I have started working on the full-on version of the game and I hope to release it this year (I'm a one-man team but I'll work doubly hard).

reviews.review_description: Love it..if anyone reads this, there were some people that had mentioned that they needed direction. This is a puzzle a game, eventually if you just take the tutorial and see how you maneuver twobit then you will get it. I thought it was fun. I can't wait for more.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your awesome feedback SuzAnne! I've been working hard on Twobit Odyssey, the full-on version which I plan to release by the end of the year (http://www.squidbeamgames.com/twobitodyssey). So there's a lot more coming for sure :)

reviews.review_description: Despite the glitch
Take your time and make a lengthy game with different characters levels with this concept n u gotta superhit game I will buy for sure!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your awesome feedback! I have received a lot of positive comments about Twobit, so I have decided to start working on the full game. More to come soon! Thanks!!!

reviews.review_description: The game glitches sometimes, but I'm forgiving the bugs because it's basically a free demo version. Looking forward to the full game.

This genre of game suits VR really well and TwoBit is a worthy example. Great graphics and immersion with a cute robot. But despite several efforts I just couldn't figure out how to solve the puzzle, so the full game would likely be too frustrating for me to consider buying. So, some hints or a walkthrough would be very welcome.

developer_response.body: Thank yoooou! I just updated Twobit so it now shows what Twobit Odyssey (the full version of the game) has in store. This demo now features three levels for free! I have also improved the tutorial and the game gives you hints on how to solve each level. You should definitively give it a try :)

reviews.review_description: Cool rolling ball bot!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your feedback! As a dev, although negative feedbacks are welcome and help me improve the game, I cherish positive ones so I know I'm doing something right :) Thank you!!!

reviews.review_description: This could be a really exciting platform game (or at least demo). It has a nice concept and good graphics and sound, but controls are flaky and gameplay is excruciatingly slow. The puzzles are also not very "logic", so you need to go through some trial-and-error to complete the demo. OTOH, it seems the concept could flourish with a bit more polishing.
Was challenging and fun to play. Really hoping more levels get added and a deeper story.

Love the hands free play, I always love puzzle games. Just frustrated there isn't more yet. (Totally understand, this was a beta test) Super excited for the full version to release in the future. Maybe a super easy level or something like that for kids would be cool. Either way I'm buying the full version.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your great feedback! I'm so happy that you like the game! As a matter of fact, I just released today the full-on version of the game called Twobit Odyssey! It features eight levels, four different environments and four bosses. It is now available in the Oculus Store and you can find it in the games section (or new releases).

reviews.review_description:
I love this game, but... 

... the time limit is too short. I feel it is unfair that the player's performance is measured by a very slow moving robot over which the player has no control to speed it up. 

It is very difficult to give commands with a headset especially if two command points are close to each other the selector rapidly jumps between the two points which is extremely frustrating.

I would love to give Twobit commands while viewing through his camera for places where Twobit is hidden. 

What is the point of that monster thing at the end?

developer_response.body: Thank you for your great feedback! I will definitively include your notes for future updates and will tune up the gaze control. The large monster at the end is part of the game's story - I've started working on the full game now that I have received positive feedback about Twobit. So the final game will give all the answers :)

reviews.review_description: I'm having the same sound issues as others have mentioned.  Using a Galaxy S7 and Gear vr,  with wired headphones.
The bot is cute! Sometimes I get stuck and hope for more hints. But overall, very fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you! You can use the Twobit vision in the game to get more hints on how to solve the levels. Twobit can see things that you can't, and his input can really help you solve the rooms! The full game (called Twobit Odyssey) features eight levels, four different environments and four different bosses! Thanks again for your awesome feedback! :)

reviews.review_description:
Hi  our good friend , dear developer
Graphic and the bot are good and the game idea is very fantastic , but no map or plan and short duration of each level made it difficult for a lot of gamer,   I hope  it will be better in full level game , probably !
